title: Brain server v2: pi_daemon grows an HTTP+SSE interface with conversation routing
current_state: pi_daemon.py (T-063) already keeps one PiAgent warm over newline-JSON TCP on 127.0.0.1:7711 — but it is single-conversation, single-client, and TCP-only, so only pi.py can talk to it. Web UI / extension / Telegram each would need their own protocol.
target_state: FastAPI app inside the daemon process: POST /chat {conversation_id?, text} → {conversation_id, response} plus GET /chat/stream (SSE) for token streaming (T-178); GET /conversations (list), GET /health. Bearer token auth from .env (PI_SERVER_TOKEN), bind 127.0.0.1 only. A per-conversation asyncio lock serializes turns within a conversation; different conversations queue through the single agent (one brain, fair FIFO). Existing TCP protocol kept for pi.py back-compat until T-188 ports it.
migration_plan: 1. app/server.py: FastAPI + uvicorn on 127.0.0.1:7712, mounted from pi_daemon (one process, one agent).
2. /chat switches active conversation: persist current, load requested (T-186 load path), run process_input, persist.
3. Global turn queue (the agent is single-threaded by design) — FIFO with per-request timeout 120s.
4. Auth middleware: constant-time token compare; 401 otherwise. Localhost bind is the hard boundary.
5. Tests: TestClient — two interleaved conversations don't bleed context (the T-142 invariant, now cross-channel); auth rejects; concurrent posts serialize.
risk_notes: The context-bleed test is the whole point — this is T-142's invariant promoted to a network boundary. requirements.txt addition (fastapi, uvicorn) — mention to Ash. Never bind 0.0.0.0 without a separate ticket + auth review.
